directive.title: Mailgun email notification on RFQ status change

auditor.summary: The build implements the Mailgun email notification module for RFQ status transitions with correct fire-and-forget pattern, proper audit trail integration, and SOC 2 compliant email redaction. The core functionality meets most acceptance criteria, but there are gaps in integration testing coverage (no end-to-end tests for audit logging behavior in the route handler) and the test framework uses vitest instead of the project's jest setup. The code structure is sound, types are properly declared, and security considerations (API key redaction, email redaction) are addressed. The build is conditionally acceptable with noted fixes for testing infrastructure and minor improvements to error handling robustness.

top_issues.description: The route handler imports from '@/lib/db' but the repo has both lib/db/index.ts and lib/db.ts? The import path '@/lib/db' would resolve to lib/db.ts if it exists, or lib/db/index.ts. The repo ground truth shows lib/db/index.ts exists, but there is no lib/db.ts file. The import '@/lib/db' is ambiguous and may cause a module resolution error if both lib/db.ts and lib/db/ directory exist. However, since only lib/db/index.ts exists, this should resolve correctly. No issue here upon closer inspection.

The route handler uses 'void (async () => { ... })()' pattern which is correct for fire-and-forget, but the email call inside uses sendRFQStatusEmail which has its own 8000ms AbortController timeout. However, the route handler does not use AbortController for the overall fire-and-forget operation. If the email call hangs (not timeout, but never resolves), the async IIFE will remain pending until the Vercel function timeout, potentially causing resource leaks.

The function sanitizes error messages by replacing MAILGUN_API_KEY with '[REDACTED]', but this is a best-effort approach. If the Mailgun API returns the API key in a different format or encoding, the regex replacement may not catch it. The spec requires MAILGUN_API_KEY to be 'provably absent from all persisted data'.
